slides.title: The 4 Main Exam Questions
slides.text:
In hard exams, you won't just count numbers. You will solve for letters like k, m, or y.

There are 4 types of questions. Let's learn to spot them.

slides.title: Type 1: The 'Whole Circle' Rule
slides.text:
Clue: Question says "n(A) = 24".

Logic: This means the Entire Circle A adds up to 24. Do not forget the intersection!

Equation: (Left Only) + (Middle) = 24
2k + (k+3) = 24

slides.title: Type 2: 'Only One' Activity
slides.text:
Clue: "36 pupils do only one activity".

Logic: This refers to the 'ears' of the diagram. We ignore the middle.

Equation: (Left Only) + (Right Only) = 36
4k + (2k+6) = 36

slides.title: Type 3: Equal Sets
slides.text:
Clue: "n(L) = n(E)".

Logic: The total of the Left Circle equals the total of the Right Circle.

Equation: (Left + Mid) = (Mid + Right)
5m+4 + m = m + 3m+12

slides.title: Type 4: The Universal Sum
slides.text:
Clue: "Total class = 90" (and there are outsiders).

Logic: Add EVERYTHING. Left + Middle + Right + Outside = Total.

Equation: (3h+1) + 2h + (h-4) + (h+2) = 90

slides.title: Final Step: Substitution
slides.text:
Finding x is not the end!

If question asks: "How many do Dancing?", you must substitute x back into the expression.

If x=5, Dancing = 4(5) + (5+2) = 27.
